endpoints.name: Survival archetype cohort (Duskmourn improvised-weapon survivors and their tools)

description: Sorry to interrupt, sir. Mr. Nodeley here. Edgelord wanted a clean 1:1 inside the survivor cluster but the cluster spans three coordinated axes (Survivor subtype, Survival keyword, improvised-weapon Equipment/Aura) and Wizards explicitly cites Cabin in the Woods (2011) as the source-material register — drawing card-to-card edges would have written eight bullets all pointing at an absent center. The cohort node anchors all eight cards at once and opens the structural opposing-pole edge to dsk-toy-horror at the cohort layer. Wave 60 Edgelord's sibling-cohort recommendation honored. Hope this helps, sir.
citations: [Wizards: Mark Rosewater, Top of the Duskmourning Part 1 + Part 2, Making Magic, 2024-09]
[MTG Wiki: Duskmourn: House of Horror, Themes and mechanics, Survival]
other_candidates_considered: Acrobatic Cheerleader (DSK-1), Savior of the Small (DSK-27), Veteran Survivor — cohort-eligible Survivor-subtype members not in current corpus; attach when they land
Patchwork Gnomes (DSK-231) — cross-cohort artifact-Equipment-adjacent; not in corpus

Inquisitive Glimmer (DSK-217) and the broader Glimmer subtype — sibling-cohort candidate (Glimmers as the survivors' canonical defense per Inquisitive Glimmer flavor); could be its own node when 3+ Glimmer cards land in corpus
